description: Cross-language CONTENT-fingerprint contract for an operator-variant's variant_label (Phase 5). dag-ml stamps this hex sha256 on every operator-SELECT RegressionMetricReport (OperatorVariantModel.variant_labels); the nirs4all host recomputes the SAME bytes from its own operator-choice config to map a per-variant report back to its config. The canonical form below is the strict contract: drift on either side breaks the mapping.

canonical_form.definition: variant_label = lowercase hex sha256 of serde_json::to_vec(canonical_value). canonical_value is a JSON array of the lowered operator sub-sequence's steps, in step order. Each step is the object {"kind": <step-kind str>, "class": <operator FQN str>, "params": {<params>}}.
canonical_form.kind: the lowered step's serde tag (snake_case): transform | y_transform | tag | exclude | filter | sample_filter | augmentation | feature_augmentation | sample_augmentation | data_generation | concat_transform | model | tuner | branch | generator | sequential | merge | merge_model | chart.
canonical_form.class: the step's operator FQN as a string. A bare-string operator ("SNV") renders to itself; any other operator value (an object like {"class": "..."}) renders to its COMPACT CANONICAL JSON text: json.dumps(operator, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":")). Structural steps that carry no operator (concat_transform, merge, branch, generator, sequential) render class as the empty string.
canonical_form.params: the step's params map, carried VERBATIM (the value forms as the DSL holds them: 1 is not coerced to 1.0). Structural steps without params render {}.
canonical_form.key_ordering: object keys sorted everywhere (the value is built from BTreeMap-backed maps; serialize with sort_keys=True on the host).
canonical_form.numeric_policy: numbers must be finite (no NaN / Inf — rejected); a bool is NOT a number; integer vs float value forms are preserved, never coerced.
canonical_form.digest: lowercase hex sha256 of the UTF-8 bytes of the canonical value serialized with no extra whitespace (serde_json::to_vec).

host_helper.note: The nirs4all host MUST call this PyO3 helper (NOT a pure-Python json.dumps + sha256) to compute variant_label for each operator-choice config. The helper shares the EXACT canonicalization + serde_json::to_vec (ryu) codepath dag-ml uses to stamp reports, so the host label is byte-identical to the report label by construction. Pure-Python json.dumps float formatting diverges from Rust (1e-05, 1e-7, 1-ULP shortest decimals — all common NIRS params), so re-deriving in Python would SILENTLY mismatch. Pass the lowered operator sub-sequence as a JSON array of PipelineDslStep objects (coerce numpy scalars via .item() / arrays via .tolist() first; non-JSON params are rejected).

consumer.note: Call dag_ml.canonical_operator_variant_label on each operator-choice config and match it against the per-variant RegressionMetricReport.variant_label to recover the choice (no positional zip).
